NKJV.1: And you He made alive, who were dead in trespasses and sins,
NKJV.2: in which you once walked according to the course of this world, according to the prince of the power of the air, the spirit who now works in the sons of disobedience,
NKJV.3: among whom also we all once conducted ourselves in the lusts of our flesh, fulfilling the desires of the flesh and of the mind, and were by nature children of wrath, just as the others.
NKJV.4: But God, who is rich in mercy, because of His great love with which He loved us,
NKJV.5: even when we were dead in trespasses, made us alive together with Christ (by grace you have been saved),
NKJV.6: and raised us up together, and made us sit together in the heavenly places in Christ Jesus,
NKJV.7: that in the ages to come He might show the exceeding riches of His grace in His kindness toward us in Christ Jesus.
NKJV.8: For by grace you have been saved through faith, and that not of yourselves; it is the gift of God,
NKJV.9: not of works, lest anyone should boast.
NKJV.10: For we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand that we should walk in them.
NKJV.11: Therefore remember that you, once Gentiles in the flesh—who are called Uncircumcision by what is called the Circumcision made in the flesh by hands—
NKJV.12: that at that time you were without Christ, being aliens from the commonwealth of Israel and strangers from the covenants of promise, having no hope and without God in the world.
NKJV.13: But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far off have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
NKJV.14: For He Himself is our peace, who has made both one, and has broken down the middle wall of separation,
NKJV.15: having abolished in His flesh the enmity, that is, the law of commandments contained in ordinances, so as to create in Himself one new man from the two, thus making peace,
NKJV.16: and that He might reconcile them both to God in one body through the cross, thereby putting to death the enmity.
NKJV.17: And He came and preached peace to you who were afar off and to those who were near.
NKJV.18: For through Him we both have access by one Spirit to the Father.
NKJV.19: Now, therefore, you are no longer strangers and foreigners, but fellow citizens with the saints and members of the household of God,
NKJV.20: having been built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ Himself being the chief cornerstone,
NKJV.21: in whom the whole building, being fitted together, grows into a holy temple in the Lord,
NKJV.22: in whom you also are being built together for a dwelling place of God in the Spirit.

ESV.1: And you were dead in the trespasses and sins
ESV.2: in which you once walked, following the course of this world, following the prince of the power of the air, the spirit that is now at work in the sons of disobedience —
ESV.3: among whom we all once lived in the passions of our flesh, carrying out the desires of the body  and the mind, and were by nature children of wrath, like the rest of mankind.
ESV.4: But  God, being rich in mercy, because of the great love with which he loved us,
ESV.5: even when we were dead in our trespasses, made us alive together with Christ — by grace you have been saved —
ESV.6: and raised us up with him and seated us with him in the heavenly places in Christ Jesus,
ESV.7: so that in the coming ages he might show the immeasurable riches of his grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus.
ESV.8: For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God,
ESV.9: not a result of works, so that no one may boast.
ESV.10: For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand, that we should walk in them.
ESV.11: Therefore remember that at one time you Gentiles in the flesh, called “the uncircumcision” by what is called the circumcision, which is made in the flesh by hands —
ESV.12: remember that you were at that time separated from Christ, alienated from the commonwealth of Israel and strangers to the covenants of promise, having no hope and without God in the world.
ESV.13: But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far off have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
ESV.14: For he himself is our peace, who has made us both one and has broken down in his flesh the dividing wall of hostility
ESV.15: by abolishing the law of commandments expressed in ordinances, that he might create in himself one new man in place of the two, so making peace,
ESV.16: and might reconcile us both to God in one body through the cross, thereby killing the hostility.
ESV.17: And he came and preached peace to you who were far off and peace to those who were near.
ESV.18: For through him we both have access in one Spirit to the Father.
ESV.19: So then you are no longer strangers and aliens,  but you are fellow citizens with the saints and members of the household of God,
ESV.20: built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Christ Jesus himself being the cornerstone,
ESV.21: in whom the whole structure, being joined together, grows into a holy temple in the Lord.
ESV.22: In him you also are being built together into a dwelling place for God by  the Spirit.

NASB.1: And you were dead in your trespasses and sins,
NASB.2: in which you formerly walked according to the course of this world, according to the prince of the power of the air, of the spirit that is now working in the sons of disobedience.
NASB.3: Among them we too all formerly lived in the lusts of our flesh, indulging the desires of the flesh and of the mind, and were by nature children of wrath, even as the rest.
NASB.4: But God, being rich in mercy, because of His great love with which He loved us,
NASB.5: even when we were dead in our transgressions, made us alive together with Christ (by grace you have been saved),
NASB.6: and raised us up with Him, and seated us with Him in the heavenly [places] in Christ Jesus,
NASB.7: so that in the ages to come He might show the surpassing riches of His grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus.
NASB.8: For by grace you have been saved through faith; and that not of yourselves, [it is] the gift of God;
NASB.9: not as a result of works, so that no one may boast.
NASB.10: For we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand so that we would walk in them.
NASB.11: Therefore remember that formerly you, the Gentiles in the flesh, who are called 'Uncircumcision ' by the so-called 'Circumcision,' [which is] performed in the flesh by human hands--
NASB.12: [remember] that you were at that time separate from Christ, excluded from the commonwealth of Israel, and strangers to the covenants of promise, having no hope and without God in the world.
NASB.13: But now in Christ Jesus you who formerly were far off have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
NASB.14: For He Himself is our peace, who made both [groups into] one and broke down the barrier of the dividing wall,
NASB.15: by abolishing in His flesh the enmity, [which is] the Law of commandments [contained] in ordinances, so that in Himself He might make the two into one new man, [thus] establishing peace,
NASB.16: and might reconcile them both in one body to God through the cross, by it having put to death the enmity.
NASB.17: AND HE CAME AND PREACHED PEACE TO YOU WHO WERE FAR AWAY, AND PEACE TO THOSE WHO WERE NEAR;
NASB.18: for through Him we both have our access in one Spirit to the Father.
NASB.19: So then you are no longer strangers and aliens, but you are fellow citizens with the saints, and are of God's household,
NASB.20: having been built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Christ Jesus Himself being the corner [stone],
NASB.21: in whom the whole building, being fitted together, is growing into a holy temple in the Lord,
NASB.22: in whom you also are being built together into a dwelling of God in the Spirit.

NLT.1: Once you were dead because of your disobedience and your many sins.
NLT.2: You used to live in sin, just like the rest of the world, obeying the devil — the commander of the powers in the unseen world. He is the spirit at work in the hearts of those who refuse to obey God.
NLT.3: All of us used to live that way, following the passionate desires and inclinations of our sinful nature. By our very nature we were subject to God’s anger, just like everyone else.
NLT.4: But God is so rich in mercy, and he loved us so much,
NLT.5: that even though we were dead because of our sins, he gave us life when he raised Christ from the dead. (It is only by God’s grace that you have been saved!)
NLT.6: For he raised us from the dead along with Christ and seated us with him in the heavenly realms because we are united with Christ Jesus.
NLT.7: So God can point to us in all future ages as examples of the incredible wealth of his grace and kindness toward us, as shown in all he has done for us who are united with Christ Jesus.
NLT.8: God saved you by his grace when you believed. And you can’t take credit for this; it is a gift from God.
NLT.9: Salvation is not a reward for the good things we have done, so none of us can boast about it.
NLT.10: For we are God’s masterpiece. He has created us anew in Christ Jesus, so we can do the good things he planned for us long ago.
NLT.11: Don’t forget that you Gentiles used to be outsiders. You were called “uncircumcised heathens” by the Jews, who were proud of their circumcision, even though it affected only their bodies and not their hearts.
NLT.12: In those days you were living apart from Christ. You were excluded from citizenship among the people of Israel, and you did not know the covenant promises God had made to them. You lived in this world without God and without hope.
NLT.13: But now you have been united with Christ Jesus. Once you were far away from God, but now you have been brought near to him through the blood of Christ.
NLT.14: For Christ himself has brought peace to us. He united Jews and Gentiles into one people when, in his own body on the cross, he broke down the wall of hostility that separated us.
NLT.15: He did this by ending the system of law with its commandments and regulations. He made peace between Jews and Gentiles by creating in himself one new people from the two groups.
NLT.16: Together as one body, Christ reconciled both groups to God by means of his death on the cross, and our hostility toward each other was put to death.
NLT.17: He brought this Good News of peace to you Gentiles who were far away from him, and peace to the Jews who were near.
NLT.18: Now all of us can come to the Father through the same Holy Spirit because of what Christ has done for us.
NLT.19: So now you Gentiles are no longer strangers and foreigners. You are citizens along with all of God’s holy people. You are members of God’s family.
NLT.20: Together, we are his house, built on the foundation of the apostles and the prophets. And the cornerstone is Christ Jesus himself.
NLT.21: We are carefully joined together in him, becoming a holy temple for the Lord.
NLT.22: Through him you Gentiles are also being made part of this dwelling where God lives by his Spirit.

WEB.1: You were made alive when you were dead in transgressions and sins,
WEB.2: in which you once walked according to the course of this world, according to the prince of the power of the air, the spirit who now works in the children of disobedience;
WEB.3: among whom we also all once lived in the lusts of our flesh, doing the desires of the flesh and of the mind, and were by nature children of wrath, even as the rest.
WEB.4: But God, being rich in mercy, for his great love with which he loved us,
WEB.5: even when we were dead through our trespasses, made us alive together with Christ (by grace you have been saved),
WEB.6: and raised us up with him, and made us to sit with him in the heavenly places in Christ Jesus,
WEB.7: that in the ages to come he might show the exceeding riches of his grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus;
WEB.8: for by grace you have been saved through faith, and that not of yourselves; it is the gift of God,
WEB.9: not of works, that no one would boast.
WEB.10: For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared before that we would walk in them.
WEB.11: Therefore remember that once you, the Gentiles in the flesh, who are called “uncircumcision” by that which is called “circumcision”, (in the flesh, made by hands);
WEB.12: that you were at that time separate from Christ, alienated from the commonwealth of Israel, and strangers from the covenants of the promise, having no hope and without God in the world.
WEB.13: But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far off are made near in the blood of Christ.
WEB.14: For he is our peace, who made both one, and broke down the middle wall of partition,
WEB.15: having abolished in his flesh the hostility, the law of commandments contained in ordinances, that he might create in himself one new man of the two, making peace;
WEB.16: and might reconcile them both in one body to God through the cross, having killed the hostility thereby.
WEB.17: He came and preached peace to you who were far off and to those who were near.
WEB.18: For through him we both have our access in one Spirit to the Father.
WEB.19: So then you are no longer strangers and foreigners, but you are fellow citizens with the saints, and of the household of God,
WEB.20: being built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Christ Jesus himself being the chief cornerstone;
WEB.21: in whom the whole building, fitted together, grows into a holy temple in the Lord;
WEB.22: in whom you also are built together for a habitation of God in the Spirit.

CSB17.1: And you were dead <sup>ⓔ</sup> in your trespasses and sins
CSB17.2: in which you previously lived according to the ways of this world, <sup>ⓕ</sup> according to the ruler of the power of the air, <sup>ⓖ</sup> the spirit <sup>ⓗ</sup> now working in the disobedient. <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.3: We too all previously lived among them in our fleshly <sup>ⓘ</sup> desires, carrying out the inclinations <sup>ⓙ</sup> of our flesh and thoughts, and we were by nature children under wrath <sup>ⓚ</sup> as the others were also.
CSB17.4: But God, who is rich in mercy, <sup>ⓛ</sup> because of his great love <sup>ⓜ</sup> that he had for us, <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.5: made us alive <sup>ⓝ</sup> with Christ even though we were dead <sup>ⓐ</sup> in trespasses. You are saved by grace!
CSB17.6: He also raised us up with him and seated us with him in the heavens in Christ Jesus, <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.7: so that in the coming ages <sup>ⓒ</sup> he might display the immeasurable riches <sup>ⓓ</sup> of his grace through his kindness <sup>ⓔ</sup> to us in Christ Jesus.
CSB17.8: For you are saved by grace <sup>ⓕ</sup> through faith, and this is not from yourselves; it is God’s gift —
CSB17.9: not from works, so that no one can boast.
CSB17.10: For we are his workmanship, created <sup>ⓖ</sup> in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared ahead of time <sup>ⓗ</sup> for us to do.
CSB17.11: So then, remember that at one time you were Gentiles in the flesh — called “the uncircumcised” by those called “the circumcised,” <sup>ⓘ</sup> which is done in the flesh by human hands. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.12: At that time you were without Christ, excluded from the citizenship of Israel, and foreigners to the covenants of promise, <sup>ⓚ</sup> without hope <sup>ⓛ</sup> and without God in the world.
CSB17.13: But now in Christ Jesus, you who were far away have been brought near by the blood <sup>ⓜ</sup> of Christ.
CSB17.14: For he is our peace, who made both groups one <sup>ⓝ</sup> and tore down the dividing wall of hostility. In his flesh,
CSB17.15: he made of no effect the law consisting of commands and expressed in regulations, so that he might create <sup>ⓐ</sup> in himself one <sup>ⓑ</sup> new man from the two, resulting in peace.
CSB17.16: He did this so that he might reconcile both to God in one body <sup>ⓒ</sup> through the cross by which he put the hostility to death. <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.17: He came and proclaimed the good news <sup>ⓓ</sup> of peace to you who were far away and peace to those who were near. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.18: For through him we both have access <sup>ⓕ</sup> in one spirit to the Father. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.19: So then you are no longer foreigners and strangers, but fellow citizens with the saints, and members of God’s household,
CSB17.20: built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, <sup>ⓗ</sup> with Christ Jesus himself as the cornerstone.
CSB17.21: In him the whole building, being put together, <sup>ⓘ</sup> grows into a holy temple in the Lord. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.22: In him you are also being built together <sup>ⓚ</sup> for God’s dwelling <sup>ⓛ</sup> in the Spirit.

AMP.1: And you [He made alive when you] were [spiritually] dead and separated from Him because of your transgressions and sins,
AMP.2: in which you once walked. You were following the ways of this world [influenced by this present age], in accordance with the prince of the power of the air (Satan), the spirit who is now at work in the disobedient [the unbelieving, who fight against the purposes of God].
AMP.3: Among these [unbelievers] we all once lived in the passions of our flesh [our behavior governed by the sinful self], indulging the desires of human nature [without the Holy Spirit] and [the impulses] of the [sinful] mind. We were, by nature, children [under the sentence] of [God's] wrath, just like the rest [of mankind].
AMP.4: But God, being [so very] rich in mercy, because of His great and wonderful love with which He loved us,
AMP.5: even when we were [spiritually] dead and separated from Him because of our sins, He made us [spiritually] alive together with Christ (for by His grace—His undeserved favor and mercy—you have been saved from God's judgment).
AMP.6: And He raised us up together with Him [when we believed], and seated us with Him in the heavenly places, [because we are] in Christ Jesus,
AMP.7: [and He did this] so that in the ages to come He might [clearly] show the immeasurable and unsurpassed riches of His grace in [His] kindness toward us in Christ Jesus [by providing for our redemption].
AMP.8: For it is by grace [God's remarkable compassion and favor drawing you to Christ] that you have been saved [actually delivered from judgment and given eternal life] through faith. And this [salvation] is not of yourselves [not through your own effort], but it is the [undeserved, gracious] gift of God;
AMP.9: not as a result of [your] works [nor your attempts to keep the Law], so that no one will [be able to] boast or take credit in any way [for his salvation].
AMP.10: For we are His workmanship [His own master work, a work of art], created in Christ Jesus [reborn from above—spiritually transformed, renewed, ready to be used] for good works, which God prepared [for us] beforehand [taking paths which He set], so that we would walk in them [living the good life which He prearranged and made ready for us].
AMP.11: Therefore, remember that at one time you Gentiles by birth, who are called "Uncircumcision" by those who called themselves "Circumcision," [itself a mere mark] which is made in the flesh by human hands—
AMP.12: remember that at that time you were separated from Christ [excluded from any relationship with Him], alienated from the commonwealth of Israel, and strangers to the covenants of promise [with no share in the sacred Messianic promise and without knowledge of God's agreements], having no hope [in His promise] and [living] in the world without God.
AMP.13: But now [at this very moment] in Christ Jesus you who once were [so very] far away [from God] have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
AMP.14: For He Himself is our peace and our bond of unity. He who made both groups—[Jews and Gentiles]—into one body and broke down the barrier, the dividing wall [of spiritual antagonism between us],
AMP.15: by abolishing in His [own crucified] flesh the hostility caused by the Law with its commandments contained in ordinances [which He satisfied]; so that in Himself He might make the two into one new man, thereby establishing peace.
AMP.16: And [that He] might reconcile them both [Jew and Gentile, united] in one body to God through the cross, thereby putting to death the hostility.
AMP.17: And He came and preached the good news of peace to you [Gentiles] who were far away, and peace to those [Jews] who were near.
AMP.18: For it is through Him that we both have a [direct] way of approach in one Spirit to the Father.
AMP.19: So then you are no longer strangers and aliens [outsiders without rights of citizenship], but you are fellow citizens with the saints (God's people), and are [members] of God's household,
AMP.20: having been built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, with Christ Jesus Himself as the [chief] Cornerstone,
AMP.21: in whom the whole structure is joined together, and it continues [to increase] growing into a holy temple in the Lord [a sanctuary dedicated, set apart, and sacred to the presence of the Lord].
AMP.22: In Him [and in fellowship with one another] you also are being built together into a dwelling place of God in the Spirit.

MSG.1: It wasn't so long ago that you were mired in that old stagnant life of sin.
MSG.2: You let the world, which doesn't know the first thing about living, tell you how to live. You filled your lungs with polluted unbelief, and then exhaled disobedience.
MSG.3: We all did it, all of us doing what we felt like doing, when we felt like doing it, all of us in the same boat. It's a wonder God didn't lose his temper and do away with the whole lot of us.
MSG.4: Instead, immense in mercy and with an incredible love,
MSG.5: he embraced us. He took our sin-dead lives and made us alive in Christ. He did all this on his own, with no help from us!
MSG.6: Then he picked us up and set us down in highest heaven in company with Jesus, our Messiah.
MSG.7: Now God has us where he wants us, with all the time in this world and the next to shower grace and kindness upon us in Christ Jesus.
MSG.8: Saving is all his idea, and all his work. All we do is trust him enough to let him do it. It's God's gift from start to finish!
MSG.9: We don't play the major role. If we did, we'd probably go around bragging that we'd done the whole thing!
MSG.10: No, we neither make nor save ourselves. God does both the making and saving. He creates each of us by Christ Jesus to join him in the work he does, the good work he has gotten ready for us to do, work we had better be doing.
MSG.11: But don't take any of this for granted. It was only yesterday that you outsiders to God's ways
MSG.12: had no idea of any of this, didn't know the first thing about the way God works, hadn't the faintest idea of Christ. You knew nothing of that rich history of God's covenants and promises in Israel, hadn't a clue about what God was doing in the world at large.
MSG.13: Now because of Christ—dying that death, shedding that blood—you who were once out of it altogether are in on everything.
MSG.14: The Messiah has made things up between us so that we're now together on this, both non-Jewish outsiders and Jewish insiders. He tore down the wall we used to keep each other at a distance.
MSG.15: He repealed the law code that had become so clogged with fine print and footnotes that it hindered more than it helped. Then he started over. Instead of continuing with two groups of people separated by centuries of animosity and suspicion, he created a new kind of human being, a fresh start for everybody.
MSG.16: Christ brought us together through his death on the Cross. The Cross got us to embrace, and that was the end of the hostility.
MSG.17: Christ came and preached peace to you outsiders and peace to us insiders.
MSG.18: He treated us as equals, and so made us equals. Through him we both share the same Spirit and have equal access to the Father.
MSG.19: That's plain enough, isn't it? You're no longer wandering exiles. This kingdom of faith is now your home country. You're no longer strangers or outsiders. You belong here, with as much right to the name Christian as anyone. God is building a home. He's using us all—irrespective of how we got here—in what he is building.
MSG.20: He used the apostles and prophets for the foundation. Now he's using you, fitting you in brick by brick, stone by stone, with Christ Jesus as the cornerstone
MSG.21: that holds all the parts together. We see it taking shape day after day—a holy temple built by God,
MSG.22: all of us built into it, a temple in which God is quite at home.

NIV.1:
Made Alive in Christ
As for you, you were dead in your transgressions and sins,
NIV.2: in which you used to live when you followed the ways of this world and of the ruler of the kingdom of the air, the spirit who is now at work in those who are disobedient.
NIV.3: All of us also lived among them at one time, gratifying the cravings of our sinful nature and following its desires and thoughts. Like the rest, we were by nature objects of wrath.
NIV.4: But because of his great love for us, God, who is rich in mercy,
NIV.5: made us alive with Christ even when we were dead in transgressions—it is by grace you have been saved.
NIV.6: And God raised us up with Christ and seated us with him in the heavenly realms in Christ Jesus,
NIV.7: in order that in the coming ages he might show the incomparable riches of his grace, expressed in his kindness to us in Christ Jesus.
NIV.8: For it is by grace you have been saved, through faith—and this not from yourselves, it is the gift of God—
NIV.9: not by works, so that no one can boast.
NIV.10: For we are God’s workmanship, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do.
NIV.11:
One in Christ
Therefore, remember that formerly you who are Gentiles by birth and called “uncircumcised” by those who call themselves “the circumcision” (that done in the body by the hands of men)—
NIV.12: remember that at that time you were separate from Christ, excluded from citizenship in Israel and foreigners to the covenants of the promise, without hope and without God in the world.
NIV.13: But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far away have been brought near through the blood of Christ.
NIV.14: For he himself is our peace, who has made the two one and has destroyed the barrier, the dividing wall of hostility,
NIV.15: by abolishing in his flesh the law with its commandments and regulations. His purpose was to create in himself one new man out of the two, thus making peace,
NIV.16: and in this one body to reconcile both of them to God through the cross, by which he put to death their hostility.
NIV.17: He came and preached peace to you who were far away and peace to those who were near.
NIV.18: For through him we both have access to the Father by one Spirit.
NIV.19: Consequently, you are no longer foreigners and aliens, but fellow citizens with God’s people and members of God’s household,
NIV.20: built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, with Christ Jesus himself as the chief cornerstone.
NIV.21: In him the whole building is joined together and rises to become a holy temple in the Lord.
NIV.22: And in him you too are being built together to become a dwelling in which God lives by his Spirit.

NRSVCE.1: You were dead through the trespasses and sins
NRSVCE.2: in which you once lived, following the course of this world, following the ruler of the power of the air, the spirit that is now at work among those who are disobedient.
NRSVCE.3: All of us once lived among them in the passions of our flesh, following the desires of flesh and senses, and we were by nature children of wrath, like everyone else.
NRSVCE.4: But God, who is rich in mercy, out of the great love with which he loved us
NRSVCE.5: even when we were dead through our trespasses, made us alive together with Christ — by grace you have been saved —
NRSVCE.6: and raised us up with him and seated us with him in the heavenly places in Christ Jesus,
NRSVCE.7: so that in the ages to come he might show the immeasurable riches of his grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus.
NRSVCE.8: For by grace you have been saved through faith, and this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God —
NRSVCE.9: not the result of works, so that no one may boast.
NRSVCE.10: For we are what he has made us, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand to be our way of life.
NRSVCE.11: So then, remember that at one time you Gentiles by birth, called “the uncircumcision” by those who are called “the circumcision” — a physical circumcision made in the flesh by human hands —
NRSVCE.12: remember that you were at that time without Christ, being aliens from the commonwealth of Israel, and strangers to the covenants of promise, having no hope and without God in the world.
NRSVCE.13: But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far off have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
NRSVCE.14: For he is our peace; in his flesh he has made both groups into one and has broken down the dividing wall, that is, the hostility between us.
NRSVCE.15: He has abolished the law with its commandments and ordinances, that he might create in himself one new humanity in place of the two, thus making peace,
NRSVCE.16: and might reconcile both groups to God in one body through the cross, thus putting to death that hostility through it.
NRSVCE.17: So he came and proclaimed peace to you who were far off and peace to those who were near;
NRSVCE.18: for through him both of us have access in one Spirit to the Father.
NRSVCE.19: So then you are no longer strangers and aliens, but you are citizens with the saints and also members of the household of God,
NRSVCE.20: built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, with Christ Jesus himself as the cornerstone.
NRSVCE.21: In him the whole structure is joined together and grows into a holy temple in the Lord;
NRSVCE.22: in whom you also are built together spiritually into a dwelling place for God.

NET.1: And although you were dead in your transgressions and sins,
NET.2: in which you formerly lived according to this world’s present path, according to the ruler of the kingdom of the air, the ruler of the spirit that is now energizing the sons of disobedience,
NET.3: among whom all of us also formerly lived out our lives in the cravings of our flesh, indulging the desires of the flesh and the mind, and were by nature children of wrath even as the rest…
NET.4: But God, being rich in mercy, because of his great love with which he loved us,
NET.5: even though we were dead in transgressions, made us alive together with Christ — by grace you are saved! —
NET.6: and he raised us up with him and seated us with him in the heavenly realms in Christ Jesus,
NET.7: to demonstrate in the coming ages the surpassing wealth of his grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus.
NET.8: For by grace you are saved through faith, and this is not from yourselves, it is the gift of God;
NET.9: it is not from works, so that no one can boast.
NET.10: For we are his workmanship, having been created in Christ Jesus for good works that God prepared beforehand so we may do them.
NET.11: Therefore remember that formerly you, the Gentiles in the flesh — who are called “uncircumcision” by the so-called “circumcision” that is performed on the body by human hands —
NET.12: that you were at that time without the Messiah, alienated from the citizenship of Israel and strangers to the covenants of promise, having no hope and without God in the world.
NET.13: But now in Christ Jesus you who used to be far away have been brought near by the blood of Christ.
NET.14: For he is our peace, the one who made both groups into one and who destroyed the middle wall of partition, the hostility,
NET.15: when he nullified in his flesh the law of commandments in decrees. He did this to create in himself one new man out of two, thus making peace,
NET.16: and to reconcile them both in one body to God through the cross, by which the hostility has been killed.
NET.17: And he came and preached peace to you who were far off and peace to those who were near,
NET.18: so that through him we both have access in one Spirit to the Father.
NET.19: So then you are no longer foreigners and noncitizens, but you are fellow citizens with the saints and members of God’s household,
NET.20: because you have been built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, with Christ Jesus himself as the cornerstone.
NET.21: In him the whole building, being joined together, grows into a holy temple in the Lord,
NET.22: in whom you also are being built together into a dwelling place of God in the Spirit.
